ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-19-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Herp for John Greene (Petitioner/Executor).

Ms. Herp stated that this was an involved Estate, that the Tax Attorney submitted time records attached to her declaration, that the Estate had a bank account in France and valuable properties in Paris and Merlo, and that the will provided for an Executor in France, but it was not compatible with France law. Ms. Herp also stated that there are two separate sets of Beneficiaries, that there are two separate properties in France, that there were difficult issues with valuation of the properties, and that they hired a translator and a French speaking US Attorney. In addition, Ms. Herp stated that preparation of the final taxes, US taxes and fiduciary taxes took a lot of time and effort, that Kim Marois' (Tax Attorney) services were invaluable, that Ms. Marois performed legal work and was fluent in French, that they were a very skilled and efficient team, that it took a lot of time, but the outcome was very successful, and asked that the Estate please be wrapped up by June 30, 2017 to avoid filing of additional taxes. The Second and Final Account and Report, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Tim Kneis. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-29-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Herp for (executor) John Greene.

The Court asked Ms. Herp to address the issue of the itemized costs. Ms. Herp agreed that some of the costs were higher than normal stating that they were all incurred on behalf of the estate, that they were dealing with an ancillary proceeding in France and the sale of French property, that Federal Express was used to transmit documents back and forth for timely delivery and to be able to track the shipment, that many of the issues were time sensitive and that they often had to enclose pre-paid Federal Express envelopes to ensure a timely return of the documents, that the ancillary probate is not settled and that there will not be so many costs going forward, that they had to use a courier to file documents with the court as they are a small firm and that they had to hire a mobile notary who could also deal with Apostille's. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy C. Robles. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Herp for John Greene (Executor).

Ms. Herp explained that Rosanne and Marianne are not blood related to the decedent, that she located a relative (Keith Wyman Elmore) in Washington through Ancestry.com, that Keith informed her that he had never heard of the decedent and that Keith contacted her on October 22, 2014 and informed her that he may want to challenge the will. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
